title: pelargonium × leontinae
intro: Pelargonium × leontopodium, also known as Pelargonium × leontinae, is a species of flowering plant in the geranium family, native to South Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of geraniol, linalool, and other volatile oils. The health benefits of Pelargonium × leontopodium include its ability to relieve symptoms of colds and flu, such as congestion and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Pelargonium × leontopodium can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
